title: Critical vulnerability SLA tracker — unpatched 30+ days with exploited-in-the-wild indicator
exclusions: Does NOT validate compensating controls; that is a separate manual process.
evidence: Daily report 'critical_vuln_sla' writes per-host per-CVE SLA evidence to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: RA-5 vulnerability scanning — the UC produces SLA evidence for remediation timeliness.
PCI 6.3 requires addressing vulnerabilities within a defined window — 30 days for critical.
Art.21(2)(e) security in acquisition / development / maintenance — patching is the operational component.
A.8.8 management of technical vulnerabilities — SLA evidence demonstrates operation of the control.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a CVE with CVSS ≥ 9.0 or marked exploited-in-the-wild is observed on a host for more than 30 days without a remediation record, the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Hosts remediated within 30 days or hosts with an accepted-risk token (valid period) do NOT fire.

spl:
index=vuln sourcetype=tenable:vm OR sourcetype=qualys:vm OR sourcetype=rapid7:vm earliest=-60d cvss_v3_base>=9.0
| stats min(_time) as first_seen max(_time) as last_seen values(cve) as cves by host
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400,0)
| where age_days>30
| join type=outer host cves [search index=vuln action=remediated]
| where isnull(remediated_at)
| table host cves first_seen age_days
description: Continuously measures how long critical or exploited-in-the-wild vulnerabilities have persisted. Breaches are surfaced immediately and feed the evidence pack with per-host details.
value: A missed critical patch is the #1 root cause of breaches — proving remediation timeliness is the matching audit control.
implementation: (1) Ingest scanner data into Splunk via TA; (2) consume CISA KEV list into exploited_in_wild.csv; (3) run daily; (4) integrate with SOAR to auto-open remediation.
visualization: Bar chart of age buckets, table of breaches, single-value '% of critical CVEs remediated within 30 days'.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application

knownFalsePositives: Compensating controls (e.g. WAF rules) can legitimately extend the SLA; capture them in vuln_exceptions.csv.
